Website's 2010 Bitcoin Giveaway Now Worth Billions
data.description: According to PANews, a report from Cointelegraph on the X platform revealed that in 2010, a website offered an incentive of 5 BTC to each visitor who completed a simple Captcha verification. The site distributed approximately 19,700 BTC in total. At current market prices, these bitcoins are valued at nearly $2 billion.

data.title: WeChat search is connected to DeepSeek-R1 and is currently in the grayscale testing stage
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: WeChat Search is currently testing the "AI Search" function in grayscale. After entering the WeChat search page, some users can click on the "Deep Thinking" option provided by DeepSeek-R1. (Interface)

data.title: A certain website once offered 5 BTC rewards to verified users in 2010, and a total of about 19,700 BTC were given away
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Cointelegraph published an article on the X platform, revealing that a certain website provided a free reward of 5 BTC to each user who completed the "Captcha verification" in 2010. According to statistics, the website gave away a total of about 19,700 BTC, and the current market price is close to 2 billion US dollars.

data.title: GRVT Brings Top Experts Onboard to Secure and Scale 2025
data.description:
GRVT, the world's first regulated decentralized exchange (DEX), has recently announced the appointment of Chris Thomas as Head of Blockchain & Corporate Security and Suren Markosov as Head of Quantitative Research.
source: https://coincu.com/322308-grvt-brings-top-experts-onboard-to-secure-scale

data.title: Intent-based solutions can fix DeFi liquidity
data.description:
Intent-based solutions offer a promising approach to tackle DeFi’s liquidity fragmentation, simplifying crosschain interactions and enhancing user experience.
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/intent-based-solutions-can-fix-de-fi-liquidity?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound
